summary.summary:
A recollection of the narrator's first day at the University of Belgrade.
- Enrolled in the Computer Science program
- Campus was bustling with new students
- Professor gave an inspiring opening lecture about the future of computing
- The experience shaped the next four years of the narrator's life

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person recalling learning to ride a bicycle

nodes.description: Park near the narrator's house where the lesson took place

nodes.name: Bicycle
nodes.description: The bicycle the narrator learned to ride

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls learning to ride a bicycle with their grandfather at age six.
- Grandfather held the seat and ran alongside
- The moment of riding independently brought pure joy
- Crashed into a bush shortly after but it didn't diminish the experience

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who watched the solar eclipse

nodes.name: Total Solar Eclipse of 1999
nodes.description: Total solar eclipse visible from parts of Europe on August 11, 1999

nodes.name: Father
nodes.description: The narrator's father who bought special viewing glasses

nodes.name: Rooftop
nodes.description: Location where the narrator watched the eclipse

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls witnessing the total solar eclipse of August 11, 1999.
- Watched from the rooftop with special glasses bought by their father
- The sky darkened in the middle of the day and birds went silent
- The eclipse lasted only a couple of minutes but left a lasting impression

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who continues the family recipe tradition

nodes.name: Grandmother
nodes.description: The creator of the family beef stew recipe

nodes.name: Beef Stew Recipe
nodes.description: A family recipe passed down for three generations

nodes.name: Cast-Iron Pot
nodes.description: The cooking vessel used for the stew

summary.summary:
The narrator preserves their grandmother's beef stew recipe passed down for three generations.
- Secret ingredient is a splash of red wine vinegar added at the end
- Always cooked in a cast-iron pot over low heat for at least four hours
- The narrator still uses the exact recipe every winter

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who wrote their first program at age twelve

nodes.name: BASIC
nodes.description: The programming language used for the first program

nodes.name: Commodore 64
nodes.description: The home computer used to write the program

nodes.name: Number Guessing Game
nodes.description: A simple game that was the narrator's first program

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls writing their first program in BASIC on a Commodore 64 at age twelve.
- Created a simple number guessing game with 'Too high' and 'Too low' feedback
- The sense of accomplishment when it ran without errors was addictive
- This moment sparked a lifelong passion for programming

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who hiked the Tour du Mont Blanc

nodes.name: Tour du Mont Blanc
nodes.description: A 170-kilometer hiking trail through France, Italy, and Switzerland

nodes.name: Two Friends
nodes.description: Companions who hiked with the narrator

summary.summary:
The narrator hiked the Tour du Mont Blanc in the summer of 2015 with two friends.
- The 170-kilometer trail passed through France, Italy, and Switzerland over eleven days
- Views from Col de la Seigne were breathtaking
- They carried all their gear and camped along the way

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who adopted the cat

nodes.description: The adopted cat with a distinctive orange patch on her left ear

nodes.name: Local Shelter
nodes.description: The animal shelter where Luna was adopted from in 2020

summary.summary:
The narrator adopted a cat named Luna from a local shelter in 2020.
- Luna was shy at first and hid under the couch for three days
- She gradually warmed up and now sleeps on the narrator's keyboard
- Luna has a distinctive orange patch on her left ear

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who experienced the blackout

nodes.name: Northeast Blackout of 2003
nodes.description: Major power outage in August 2003 that lasted nearly two days

nodes.name: Neighbors
nodes.description: Community members who gathered during the blackout

nodes.name: Street Gathering
nodes.description: Impromptu community gathering during the blackout

summary.summary:
The narrator experienced the Northeast blackout of August 2003 which lasted nearly two days.
- Neighbors gathered on the street and shared food from their fridges
- Someone played guitar and they sang songs by candlelight
- It was a rare moment of community in an otherwise isolating city

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who learned to swim at age eight

nodes.description: Patient but firm swimming instructor

nodes.name: Municipal Pool
nodes.description: The pool where swimming lessons took place

summary.summary:
The narrator learned to swim at the municipal pool at age eight under Mrs. Petrovic's instruction.
- The instructor was patient but firm in her teaching approach
- Had to practice floating on backs before anything else
- Was terrified of the deep end for months before finally diving in

nodes.name: Narrator

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls their first real job interview at a small software consultancy in 2010.
- Was so nervous they forgot their portfolio at home
- Blanked on quicksort but successfully explained merge sort on the whiteboard
- Received a job offer two days later

summary.summary:
The narrator moved to Berlin in 2018 knowing nobody in the city.
- The first month was lonely, spent exploring neighborhoods on foot
- Found a co-working space where they met people who became close friends
- The Kreuzberg district felt like home within six months

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Ten-year-old who helped build the treehouse

summary.summary:
The narrator and their father built a treehouse in the backyard oak tree when the narrator was ten.
- Spent three weekends measuring, cutting, and nailing boards
- Featured a rope ladder and a small window facing west
- The narrator used it as a reading nook every summer until they moved

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person attending their first live concert

nodes.name: Radiohead
nodes.description: British rock band that performed at the festival

nodes.name: Music Festival
nodes.description: Festival in 2006 where Radiohead performed

nodes.name: Karma Police
nodes.description: Radiohead song the crowd sang along to

summary.summary:
The narrator's first live concert was Radiohead at a festival in 2006.
- The band opened with 'There There' and the crowd erupted
- Thousands of people sang along to 'Karma Police' in a transcendent moment
- The narrator still has the wristband from that day

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Engineer who debugged the production outage

nodes.description: Database that went down due to misconfigured connection pool

nodes.name: Connection Pool Config
nodes.description: Configuration that was changed from 100 to 10 max connections

nodes.name: Recent Deployment
nodes.description: Deployment that introduced the misconfiguration

summary.summary:
The narrator debugged a production database outage at 3 AM in 2019.
- A recent deployment had changed max connections from 100 to 10
- Spent four hours tracing the issue through logs
- The fix was a one-line config change but the investigation was grueling

nodes.name: Narrator

nodes.description: Renaissance artist who created the David sculpture

nodes.description: Museum in Florence housing the David sculpture

nodes.name: Florence
nodes.description: Italian city where the museum is located

summary.summary:
The narrator visited the Galleria dell'Accademia in Florence to see Michelangelo's David.
- No photograph prepares you for the scale and detail of the sculpture
- Stood there for twenty minutes just looking at the hands
- The museum was crowded but everything else faded away in that moment

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who made their first open source contribution

nodes.name: Python Logging Library
nodes.description: Open source Python library where the bug was fixed

nodes.name: Race Condition Bug
nodes.description: Bug in the file rotation handler that caused log file truncation

nodes.name: Pull Request
nodes.description: Code contribution that took three rounds of review

summary.summary:
The narrator's first open source contribution was a bug fix to a Python logging library in 2014.
- Fixed a race condition in the file rotation handler causing log file truncation
- The pull request went through three rounds of review before being merged
- Seeing their name in the changelog was incredibly satisfying

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who experienced the snow day

nodes.name: January 1998 Snowfall
nodes.description: The heaviest snowfall the narrator remembers, canceling school for three days

nodes.name: Snow Fort
nodes.description: Enormous snow fort with tunnels and a watchtower built in the park

nodes.name: Park
nodes.description: Location where the snow fort was built

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls the heaviest snowfall in January 1998 when school was canceled for three days.
- Built an enormous snow fort in the park with tunnels and a watchtower
- Hands were frozen but refused to go inside
- The fort survived until February before collapsing

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Fourteen-year-old who read the entire trilogy in one week

nodes.name: Lord of the Rings
nodes.description: Fantasy novel trilogy by J.R.R. Tolkien

nodes.name: J.R.R. Tolkien
nodes.description: Author of the Lord of the Rings

nodes.name: Middle-earth
nodes.description: Fictional setting of the Lord of the Rings

summary.summary:
The narrator read the entire Lord of the Rings trilogy in one week during summer break at age fourteen.
- Barely slept or ate, completely absorbed in Middle-earth
- 'The Bridge of Khazad-dum' chapter gave them chills
- First time a book made them cry, during the Grey Havens ending

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: College student who learned pasta cooking techniques

nodes.name: Italian Roommate
nodes.description: Italian college roommate who taught pasta cooking rules

nodes.name: Pasta Cooking Technique
nodes.description: Rules about salting water, not breaking spaghetti, and saving pasta water

summary.summary:
An Italian roommate in college taught the narrator essential pasta cooking rules.
- Salt the pasta water generously so it tastes like the sea
- Never break spaghetti
- Always save some pasta water for the sauce
- These simple rules transformed the narrator's cooking forever

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Team member who participated in the hackathon

nodes.name: 48-Hour Hackathon
nodes.description: Hackathon in 2017 that the narrator's team won

nodes.name: Sign Language Translator
nodes.description: Real-time translator for sign language using computer vision

nodes.name: CNN Model
nodes.description: Pre-trained convolutional neural network fine-tuned on ASL datasets

nodes.name: Team
nodes.description: Four-person team that won the hackathon

summary.summary:
The narrator's team won a 48-hour hackathon in 2017 by building a sign language translator.
- Used computer vision with a pre-trained CNN model fine-tuned on ASL datasets
- The demo translated basic signs with 85% accuracy
- The four-person team split the prize equally

memories.title: A Flat Tire on a Road Trip

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: One of the travelers on the road trip

nodes.name: Deserted Highway
nodes.description: Stretch of highway where the flat tire occurred

nodes.name: Truck Driver
nodes.description: Helpful stranger who stopped to assist with the tire change

nodes.name: Beach
nodes.description: Coastal destination of the road trip

summary.summary:
The narrator got a flat tire halfway through a road trip to the coast.
- None of the travelers had changed a tire before and used the car manual
- A truck driver stopped to help and corrected the jack position
- They arrived at the beach two hours late

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who set up their first Linux server

nodes.description: Linux server running Apache and MySQL set up in 2011

nodes.name: Apache
nodes.description: Web server software running on the Debian box

nodes.name: Personal Website
nodes.description: Website that was accessible from the internet

summary.summary:
The narrator set up their first Linux server in 2011, a Debian box running Apache and MySQL.
- Spent an entire weekend configuring iptables rules and SSH keys
- The thrill of seeing the website accessible from the internet was unmatched
- The server was hacked within a week due to default port 22 configuration

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who started a balcony garden in 2021

nodes.name: Basil
nodes.description: Basil plants that produced enough for weekly pesto

nodes.name: Peppers
nodes.description: Pepper plants that struggled with insufficient sunlight

summary.summary:
The narrator started a small vegetable garden on their balcony in spring 2021.
- Planted tomatoes, basil, and peppers in large pots
- Tomatoes thrived but peppers struggled with insufficient sunlight
- By August, harvesting enough basil to make pesto every week
- The garden became a meditative morning ritual

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who watched the World Cup final

nodes.description: Final match between Germany and Argentina

nodes.name: Germany
nodes.description: National football team that won the final

nodes.name: Mario Gotze
nodes.description: Player who scored the winning goal in extra time

summary.summary:
The narrator watched the 2014 World Cup final between Germany and Argentina.
- Gathered at a friend's apartment with snacks and drinks
- Gotze scored in extra time causing half the room to erupt
- The energy in the small living room was electric

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The mentee who learned from David

nodes.name: David
nodes.description: Senior engineer who mentored the narrator by reviewing code line by line

nodes.name: Mentorship Approach
nodes.description: Teaching methodology of asking questions rather than providing answers

summary.summary:
The narrator reflects on their first real mentor, a senior engineer named David.
- David reviewed the narrator's code line by line
- Never just told what was wrong but asked questions to lead to discovery
- Taught that good mentorship develops thinking rather than providing answers

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person at the beach during the thunderstorm

nodes.name: Beach
nodes.description: Coastal location where the storm arrived

nodes.name: Thunderstorm
nodes.description: Sudden storm that rolled in from the sea

nodes.name: Car
nodes.description: Vehicle where the narrator sheltered and watched the storm

summary.summary:
The narrator experienced a sudden thunderstorm while at the beach.
- The sky turned dark green and wind picked up in seconds
- Ran to the car as lightning struck the water offshore
- Watched the storm from inside the car, finding it both terrifying and beautiful

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who learned git through a painful mistake

nodes.name: Git

nodes.name: Pro Git Book
nodes.description: Book about git read cover to cover over a weekend

nodes.name: Git Reflog
nodes.description: Git feature that saved the narrator multiple times

summary.summary:
The narrator learned git the hard way by accidentally deleting a week's work with git reset --hard in 2013.
- Spent a weekend reading the Pro Git book cover to cover
- Understanding the reflog saved them multiple times afterward
- Now teaches git workshops to junior developers

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who started baking sourdough during lockdown

nodes.description: Type of bread the narrator learned to bake

nodes.name: Clint Yeastwood
nodes.description: The narrator's sourdough starter, named humorously

nodes.name: 2020 Lockdown
nodes.description: COVID-19 lockdown period when the narrator started baking

summary.summary:
The narrator started baking sourdough bread during the 2020 lockdown.
- The sourdough starter took two weeks to become active
- First loaf was dense and sour but improved by the fifth attempt
- Named the starter 'Clint Yeastwood'
- Eventually produced bread with a good crumb and crispy crust

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Traveler on a delayed train from Prague to Vienna

nodes.description: Physics concept explained using a coffee cup metaphor

nodes.name: Prague to Vienna Train
nodes.description: Delayed train where the conversation took place

summary.summary:
The narrator had a three-hour conversation with a retired physicist on a delayed train from Prague to Vienna.
- The physicist described his work on particle accelerators in the 1980s
- Explained quantum tunneling using a memorable coffee cup metaphor
- The conversation made the narrator reconsider assumptions about time and randomness

nodes.name: Engineering Team
nodes.description: Team that spent eight months on the migration

nodes.description: Legacy application that was migrated

nodes.name: Microservices Architecture
nodes.description: Target architecture for the migration

nodes.name: Strangler Fig Pattern
nodes.description: Migration pattern used to gradually route traffic to new services

summary.summary:
The narrator's team spent eight months in 2020 migrating a monolithic Python application to microservices.
- Used the strangler fig pattern to gradually route traffic to new services
- The hardest part was splitting the shared database
- Latency improved by 40% but operational complexity tripled

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who grew up with Rex

nodes.name: Rex
nodes.description: German Shepherd who lived with the family for thirteen years

nodes.name: Family
nodes.description: The narrator's family who owned Rex

summary.summary:
The narrator recalls their family dog Rex, a German Shepherd who lived with them for thirteen years.
- Rex waited at the door every day when the narrator came home from school
- Once chased a deer through the woods and came back three hours later covered in mud
- When Rex passed away, the house felt empty for months

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Sixteen-year-old experiencing their first flight

nodes.name: Belgrade
nodes.description: Departure city for the flight

nodes.name: London
nodes.description: Destination city for the flight

nodes.name: First Flight
nodes.description: Two-hour flight from Belgrade to London

summary.summary:
The narrator's first airplane flight was from Belgrade to London at age sixteen.
- Got a window seat and watched the clouds from above for the first time
- The takeoff made their stomach drop but leveled off into a serene view
- Pressed face against the window for the entire two-hour flight

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person learning Japanese starting in 2019

nodes.name: Japanese Language
nodes.description: Language the narrator studied using apps and textbooks

nodes.name: Tokyo
nodes.description: City visited where the narrator used Japanese to order food

nodes.name: Kanji
nodes.description: Japanese writing system that felt endless to learn

summary.summary:
The narrator started learning Japanese in 2019 using apps and textbooks.
- Hiragana took a week, katakana another week, but kanji felt endless
- After two years, could hold basic conversations
- Ordering food in Japanese for the first time in Tokyo made all study hours worth it

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Engineer who discovered the broken pipeline

nodes.name: CI/CD Pipeline
nodes.description: Continuous integration pipeline that broke silently for two weeks

nodes.name: GitHub Actions
nodes.description: CI/CD platform with the misconfigured workflow

nodes.name: Canary Test
nodes.description: Intentionally failing test added to ensure the suite executes

summary.summary:
The narrator discovered their CI/CD pipeline had been silently broken for two weeks in 2021.
- Tests were passing but not actually running due to a misconfigured GitHub Actions workflow
- Discovered when a clearly broken PR was merged to main
- Added a canary test that intentionally fails to ensure the test suite actually executes

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Customer of the neighborhood bookstore

nodes.name: Neighborhood Bookstore
nodes.description: Tiny bookstore on the narrator's street

nodes.name: Elderly Couple
nodes.description: Owners who knew every book by heart and could recommend titles

nodes.name: Neighborhood
nodes.description: Community that felt the loss when the bookstore closed

summary.summary:
The narrator fondly remembers a tiny bookstore on their street run by an elderly couple.
- The owners knew every book by heart and could recommend based on vague descriptions
- The shop closed in 2019 when the couple retired
- The whole neighborhood felt the loss

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Person who ran their first marathon in 2016

nodes.name: Vienna Marathon
nodes.description: Marathon event in Vienna where the narrator ran in 2016

nodes.name: Vienna
nodes.description: City where the marathon took place

nodes.name: Ringstrasse
nodes.description: Vienna boulevard where crowds cheered the narrator on

summary.summary:
The narrator ran their first marathon in Vienna in 2016.
- Training took five months with a gradual buildup from 5K to 42 kilometers
- Hit the wall at kilometer 35 and considered stopping
- The crowd cheering along the Ringstrasse pushed them through
- Finished in 4 hours and 12 minutes

nodes.name: Narrator

nodes.name: Pi-hole
nodes.description: DNS-level ad blocking software

nodes.name: VLANs
nodes.description: Virtual LANs separating IoT devices from work machines

summary.summary:
The narrator set up a mesh Wi-Fi network and learned more about networking than in any class.
- Configured VLANs to separate IoT devices from work machines
- Set up Pi-hole for DNS-level ad blocking
- Ran Ethernet through the walls
- Partner thought it was overcomplicated but the network has been rock-solid

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who hiked to see the sunrise

nodes.name: Dolomites
nodes.description: Mountain range in Italy where the sunrise was witnessed

nodes.name: Mountain Summit
nodes.description: Peak reached at sunrise after an early morning hike

nodes.name: Sunrise
nodes.description: Sunrise that turned rock faces orange and pink

summary.summary:
The narrator woke up at 4 AM to hike to a summit in the Dolomites to watch the sunrise.
- The trail was dark and cold with only a headlamp to guide the way
- The sun broke over the peaks turning rock faces orange and pink
- Sat alone at the summit for thirty minutes not wanting to move

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Student who had an epiphany about recursion

nodes.name: Recursion
nodes.description: Programming concept that clicked during a lecture

nodes.name: Tower of Hanoi
nodes.description: Classic problem used to demonstrate recursion

nodes.name: Recursive Maze Solver
nodes.description: Program the narrator wrote after understanding recursion

summary.summary:
The narrator describes the moment recursion clicked during a lecture on the Tower of Hanoi.
- The professor showed how the solution was the same problem on a smaller scale
- Went home and implemented it, then wrote a recursive maze solver that evening
- First time an abstract concept felt physically intuitive

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who discovered specialty coffee

nodes.name: Third-Wave Coffee Shop
nodes.description: Specialty coffee shop visited in 2017

nodes.name: Barista
nodes.description: Coffee professional who explained processing methods

nodes.name: Ethiopian Yirgacheffe
nodes.description: Coffee variety with blueberry flavor notes

summary.summary:
The narrator discovered specialty coffee at a third-wave coffee shop in 2017.
- A barista explained the difference between washed and natural processing
- Started with a pour-over of Ethiopian Yirgacheffe and tasted blueberries in coffee for the first time
- Has been a coffee enthusiast since that cup

memories.title: A Power Outage During a Demo

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person presenting the demo when power went out

nodes.name: Client
nodes.description: Client who was impressed by the quick recovery

nodes.name: Dashboard Demo
nodes.description: Client demonstration of a new dashboard in 2018

nodes.name: Construction Crew
nodes.description: Workers who cut a cable outside the building causing the outage

summary.summary:
During a client demo in 2018, the office power went out while showing a new dashboard.
- Switched to phone hotspot and continued the demo from laptop on battery
- The client was impressed by the quick recovery
- Later learned a construction crew had cut a cable outside the building

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who learned chess at age seven

nodes.description: Family member who taught chess and never let the narrator win

nodes.name: School Chess Club
nodes.description: Club the narrator joined to improve at chess

nodes.name: Regional Tournament
nodes.description: Competition where the narrator placed third

summary.summary:
The narrator's uncle taught them chess at age seven, never letting them win.
- The strict approach frustrated at first but taught genuine improvement
- Joined the school chess club and placed third in a regional tournament
- Still plays online most evenings as a way to decompress

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The developer who first used Docker in 2015

nodes.name: Docker
nodes.description: Container platform that felt like magic to use

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.description: Fifteen-line configuration for a Python app

nodes.name: Development Team
nodes.description: Team the narrator evangelized Docker to

summary.summary:
The narrator first used Docker in 2015 and found it transformative.
- Went from 'it works on my machine' to reproducible environments in an afternoon
- The Dockerfile for the Python app was fifteen lines and solved months of deployment headaches
- Became the Docker evangelist on the team for the next year

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Volunteer at the food bank every Saturday for six months

nodes.name: Local Food Bank
nodes.description: Community organization where donations were sorted and packed

nodes.name: Families in Need
nodes.description: Recipients of the packed food boxes

summary.summary:
The narrator volunteered at a local food bank every Saturday for six months in 2019.
- Sorted donations and packed boxes for families in need
- Managing perishable items taught more about supply chain than any business course
- The gratitude from recipients made the early mornings worthwhile

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person stargazing during a camping trip

nodes.name: Milky Way
nodes.description: Galaxy clearly visible for the first time far from city lights

nodes.name: Friend
nodes.description: Companion who pointed out constellations

nodes.name: Constellations
nodes.description: Star patterns including Orion, Cassiopeia, and the Big Dipper

summary.summary:
The narrator saw the Milky Way clearly for the first time during a countryside camping trip.
- Far from city lights, the sheer number of visible stars was overwhelming
- A friend pointed out Orion, Cassiopeia, and the Big Dipper
- Stayed up until 3 AM watching satellites and saw two shooting stars

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Engineer who spent three months refactoring legacy code

nodes.name: 10,000-Line PHP File
nodes.description: Legacy code file accumulating features for eight years

nodes.name: Characterization Tests
nodes.description: Tests written to lock down existing behavior before refactoring

nodes.name: Engineering Team
nodes.description: Team that could safely modify the code after refactoring

summary.summary:
The narrator spent three months refactoring a 10,000-line PHP file that had accumulated features for eight years.
- First step was writing characterization tests to lock down existing behavior
- Extracted classes one at a time, running the test suite after every change
- The code ended up in twenty well-named files the team could safely modify

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: Tourist who visited Kyoto's bamboo grove in the rain

nodes.name: Kyoto
nodes.description: Japanese city visited by the narrator

nodes.name: Bamboo Grove
nodes.description: Famous bamboo forest in Kyoto visited in the rain

nodes.name: Rain
nodes.description: All-day rain that drove most tourists away

summary.summary:
The narrator visited Kyoto's bamboo grove on a day when it rained the entire time.
- Most tourists left but the narrator stayed and walked through almost alone
- The sound of rain on bamboo leaves created a natural symphony
- The green was more vivid in the wet light, making bad weather create the best memories

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who taught their cousin Python

nodes.description: Cousin who learned Python and is now studying computer science

nodes.name: Python
nodes.description: Programming language taught over the summer

nodes.name: Text Adventure Game
nodes.description: Simple game built together during the lessons

summary.summary:
The narrator taught their younger cousin the basics of Python over a summer.
- Started with variables and loops, then built a simple text adventure game together
- Watching the cousin's face light up when code ran correctly was rewarding
- The cousin is now studying computer science in university

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who lost data due to a hard drive failure

nodes.name: Laptop Hard Drive
nodes.description: Hard drive that failed in 2016 taking six months of projects

nodes.name: Automated Backup System
nodes.description: External drive and cloud storage backup set up after the failure

nodes.name: Personal Projects
nodes.description: Six months of work lost due to no backup

summary.summary:
The narrator learned the importance of backups when their laptop hard drive failed in 2016.
- Lost six months of personal projects with no backup whatsoever
- Set up automated backups to an external drive and cloud storage afterward
- Adopted the rule: if it exists in only one place, it doesn't really exist

nodes.name: Narrator
nodes.description: The person who discovered podcasts during their commute

nodes.name: Podcasts
nodes.description: Audio medium discovered during daily commute in 2016

nodes.name: Technology History Series
nodes.description: First podcast the narrator binged, covering the invention of the transistor

nodes.name: Daily Commute
nodes.description: Regular travel time transformed into a learning opportunity

summary.summary:
The narrator discovered podcasts during their daily commute in 2016.
- First podcast binged was a technology history series covering the invention of the transistor
- Listening transformed the commute from wasted time into a learning opportunity
- Now listens to about ten hours of podcasts per week
